sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the bottom-right panel clearly. It's a 3x3 grid with:
- All blue cells (6x6 each)
- Charcoal (c) separators between cells (2px wide)
- A gray (g) border frame around the outside
- A center piece with white/gray/red pattern
The layout of the bottom-right panel:
- Rows 36-41: top row of 3 blue cells
- Rows 42-43: separator
- Rows 44-49: middle row (blue, center piece, blue)
- Rows 50-51: separator
- Rows 52-57: bottom row of 3 blue cells
- Rows 58-59: separator
- Rows 60-61: gray border bottom
The cells are 6x6 blue blocks separated by 2px charcoal lines. The center cell has the special piece.
Now I need to figure out the goal. Looking at the 4 panels:
- Top-left: blue, red, blue / red, center, blue / blue, red, red
- Top-right: blue, red, blue / red, center, red / blue, red, blue
- Bottom-left: red, blue, blue / red, center, blue / blue, blue, red
- Bottom-right: blue, blue, blue / blue, center, blue / blue, blue, blue (all blue)
The bottom-right panel is the target (has the gray border). The goal might be to change the blue blocks to match one of the patterns above, or to combine them somehow.
Let me try clicking on one of the blue blocks in the bottom-right panel to see if it toggles between blue and red. I'll click on the top-left cell of the bottom-right panel (around row 38, col 38).
Wait, actually looking at the ascii output, the bottom-right panel starts at col 34 (where the 'c' chars begin). The first blue cell starts at col 36 (after the 2px charcoal border).
Let me try clicking on a blue cell in the bottom-right panel.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on a blue block in the bottom-right panel
# The blue cells are 6x6. Top-left cell: rows 36-41, cols 36-41
# Let me click at the center of that cell: row 38, col 38
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 38}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
